decks.title: Movies & TV
decks.description: From comfort rewatches to prestige TV obsessions, this deck explores the stories and screen moods you keep coming back to.

cards.title: The Shawshank Redemption
cards.subtitle: Frank Darabont, 1994
cards.description_short: A prison drama built on hope, friendship, and endurance.

cards.title: The Office (US)
cards.subtitle: NBC, 2005-2013
cards.description_short: A cozy workplace comedy full of cringe, affection, and quotable bits.

cards.title: The Lord of the Rings trilogy
cards.subtitle: Peter Jackson, 2001-2003
cards.description_short: A sweeping fantasy adventure with mythic scale and sincere emotion.

cards.title: Spirited Away
cards.subtitle: Hayao Miyazaki, 2001
cards.description_short: A dreamlike coming-of-age story with warmth, wonder, and strange magic.

cards.title: Get Out
cards.subtitle: Jordan Peele, 2017
cards.description_short: A sharp horror film that mixes dread, satire, and social tension.

cards.title: Fleabag
cards.subtitle: BBC, 2016-2019
cards.description_short: A brutally funny series about grief, desire, and self-sabotage.

cards.title: Breaking Bad
cards.subtitle: AMC, 2008-2013
cards.description_short: A tense antihero drama about power, pride, and transformation.

cards.title: Friends
cards.subtitle: NBC, 1994-2004
cards.description_short: A comfort-watch sitcom centered on friendship, routines, and romance.

cards.title: Parasite
cards.subtitle: Bong Joon-ho, 2019
cards.description_short: A thrilling class satire that shifts from funny to devastating.

cards.title: The Bear
cards.subtitle: FX, 2022-
cards.description_short: An intense workplace dramedy about pressure, grief, and family.

cards.title: Black Mirror
cards.subtitle: Channel 4 / Netflix, 2011-
cards.description_short: A dark anthology about technology, fear, and human weakness.

cards.title: Pride & Prejudice
cards.subtitle: Joe Wright, 2005
cards.description_short: A romantic period drama about pride, timing, and emotional restraint.

cards.title: Derry Girls
cards.subtitle: Channel 4, 2018-2022
cards.description_short: A chaotic teen comedy that turns nostalgia into something warm and sharp.

cards.title: Interstellar
cards.subtitle: Christopher Nolan, 2014
cards.description_short: A big, emotional sci-fi film about love, sacrifice, and time.

cards.title: Knives Out
cards.subtitle: Rian Johnson, 2019
cards.description_short: A playful murder mystery that balances wit, pace, and style.

cards.title: The Great British Bake Off
cards.subtitle: BBC / Channel 4, 2010-
cards.description_short: A soothing competition show built on kindness, pressure, and cake.

cards.title: The Last of Us
cards.subtitle: HBO, 2023-
cards.description_short: A survival drama that mixes violence with grief, tenderness, and fear.

cards.title: Abbott Elementary
cards.subtitle: ABC, 2021-
cards.description_short: A bright workplace comedy with heart, hustle, and affection.

cards.title: The Good Place
cards.subtitle: NBC, 2016-2020
cards.description_short: A philosophical comedy about ethics, friendship, and second chances.

cards.title: Severance
cards.subtitle: Apple TV+, 2022-
cards.description_short: A stylish corporate thriller about identity, memory, and control.

cards.title: Planet Earth
cards.subtitle: BBC, 2006
cards.description_short: A gorgeous documentary series that makes the natural world feel huge and intimate.

cards.title: Succession
cards.subtitle: HBO, 2018-2023
cards.description_short: A vicious family drama where cruelty and comedy live side by side.

cards.title: When Harry Met Sally
cards.subtitle: Rob Reiner, 1989
cards.description_short: A classic romantic comedy about timing, friendship, and emotional honesty.

cards.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
cards.subtitle: George Miller, 2015
cards.description_short: A relentless action film that feels both brutal and strangely elegant.

cards.title: Studio Ghibli
cards.subtitle: Animation studio
cards.description_short: A whole visual world associated with tenderness, imagination, and melancholy.

cards.title: The Princess Bride
cards.subtitle: Rob Reiner, 1987
cards.description_short: A playful fairy tale that stays earnest without losing its charm.

cards.title: The West Wing
cards.subtitle: NBC, 1999-2006
cards.description_short: An idealistic political drama driven by fast dialogue and moral urgency.

cards.title: Free Solo
cards.subtitle: Jimmy Chin and Elizabeth Chai Vasarhelyi, 2018
cards.description_short: A nerve-shredding documentary about risk, obsession, and mastery.

cards.title: I'd rather rewatch a favorite than try something new
cards.description_short: Shows whether comfort and familiarity usually beat novelty for you.

cards.title: I care more about writing than special effects
cards.description_short: Highlights whether story craft matters more than spectacle.

cards.title: I usually want subtitles, even when the audio is in English
cards.description_short: Reveals how much clarity and detail shape the viewing experience for you.

cards.title: I'll watch something scary if the plot feels worth it
cards.description_short: Separates interest in suspense from interest in pure comfort viewing.

cards.title: My mood matters more than the reviews when I choose what to watch
cards.description_short: Shows whether mood-fit beats consensus when you pick a title.

cards.title: I'd rather watch at home than go to a theater
cards.description_short: Reveals whether convenience and coziness beat the big-screen ritual for you.

cards.title: I love talking about a movie right after it ends
cards.description_short: Captures whether post-watch conversation is part of the fun for you.

cards.title: I'm more likely to binge a whole season than watch one episode a week
cards.description_short: Shows whether you prefer immersion over slow pacing.

cards.title: If a show has too many seasons, I hesitate to start it
cards.description_short: Reveals whether commitment fatigue affects your picks.

cards.title: I prefer slow, character-driven stories over twisty plots
cards.description_short: Highlights whether emotional texture matters more than surprise.

cards.title: Spoilers genuinely ruin the experience for me
cards.description_short: Shows how much suspense and discovery matter to your enjoyment.

cards.title: On most nights, I'd choose a cozy comedy over an intense prestige drama
cards.description_short: Separates comfort viewing from heavier emotional challenge.

decks.title: Music
decks.description: The sounds you replay, chase, and share can reveal rhythm, nostalgia, emotional openness, and energy.

cards.title: Beyoncé
cards.description_short: Big-stage pop power, precision, and emotional confidence.

cards.title: Taylor Swift
cards.description_short: A songwriter whose catalog invites loyalty, memory, and debate.

cards.title: Kendrick Lamar
cards.description_short: Dense, ambitious hip-hop with storytelling, vulnerability, and edge.

cards.title: Fleetwood Mac
cards.description_short: Classic rock full of tension, hooks, and emotional spillover.

cards.title: Frank Ocean
cards.description_short: Soft-focus R&B that rewards emotional attention and repeat listens.

cards.title: Daft Punk
cards.description_short: Electronic music with sleek nostalgia, joy, and immaculate rhythm.

cards.title: Dolly Parton
cards.description_short: Country storytelling with charm, wit, and emotional clarity.

cards.title: Nina Simone
cards.description_short: A singular voice where jazz, soul, and ache meet each other.

cards.title: Bad Bunny
cards.description_short: Global pop energy with confidence, rhythm, and movement.

cards.title: Phoebe Bridgers
cards.description_short: Indie songwriting that turns dry wit and sadness into intimacy.

cards.title: Random Access Memories
cards.subtitle: Daft Punk, 2013
cards.description_short: A glossy album made for headphones, dancing, and sonic detail.

cards.title: Rumours
cards.subtitle: Fleetwood Mac, 1977
cards.description_short: An iconic album where perfect songs barely contain messy feelings.

cards.title: To Pimp a Butterfly
cards.subtitle: Kendrick Lamar, 2015
cards.description_short: A maximal, political, deeply personal hip-hop landmark.

cards.title: Golden Hour
cards.subtitle: Kacey Musgraves, 2018
cards.description_short: A warm country-pop album built for softness, glow, and ease.

cards.title: The Miseducation of Lauryn Hill
cards.subtitle: Lauryn Hill, 1998
cards.description_short: A soulful record that mixes confidence, heartbreak, and vision.

cards.title: In Rainbows
cards.subtitle: Radiohead, 2007
cards.description_short: A restless rock album that feels intimate, strange, and alive.

cards.title: Brandi Carlile
cards.description_short: Big-hearted songwriting with grit, warmth, and live appeal.

cards.title: House music
cards.description_short: Rhythm-first electronic music made for release, repetition, and movement.

cards.title: Jazz standards
cards.description_short: A shared songbook where interpretation matters as much as melody.

cards.title: SZA
cards.description_short: Modern R&B full of confession, edge, and melodic drift.

cards.title: Bon Iver
cards.description_short: Layered indie music that feels reflective, cold-weather, and transportive.

cards.title: Tiny Desk concerts
cards.description_short: Intimate performances that reveal how much an artist can really do live.

cards.title: Karaoke nights
cards.description_short: A social music ritual where confidence and chaos usually meet.

cards.title: Vinyl listening
cards.description_short: A slower ritual where sequencing and attention feel part of the point.

cards.title: I sing in the car even when I don't know all the words
cards.description_short: Shows whether music is something you feel physically, not just listen to.

cards.title: Lyrics matter more to me than production
cards.description_short: Highlights whether you connect through words before sound design.

cards.title: I like having music on while I cook, clean, or reset my space
cards.description_short: Reveals whether sound is woven into your ordinary routines.

cards.title: I'd rather listen to a full album than a shuffled playlist
cards.description_short: Shows whether sequencing and intent matter to your listening experience.

cards.title: Live shows make me appreciate an artist more
cards.description_short: Separates headphone love from performance love.

cards.title: I enjoy finding new music more than replaying old favorites
cards.description_short: Captures whether novelty or familiarity usually drives your listening.

cards.title: I make playlists for very specific moods
cards.description_short: Shows whether music is part of how you manage energy and feeling.

cards.title: I'm happy to let someone else control the road-trip playlist
cards.description_short: Reveals how protective or flexible you are around shared listening.

cards.title: I notice bass lines and production details more than most people do
cards.description_short: Highlights whether your ear goes to texture, layering, and rhythm first.

cards.title: Calming music usually wins over hype music at home
cards.description_short: Shows whether your default listening style is soothing or energizing.

cards.title: I love a good karaoke night even if my voice is questionable
cards.description_short: Captures whether music feels participatory and social for you.

cards.title: I usually know within 30 seconds whether a song is for me
cards.description_short: Reveals whether your taste feels immediate or slower to unfold.

cards.title: I prefer intimate venues over giant arenas
cards.description_short: Shows whether closeness and detail beat scale and spectacle for you.

cards.title: I can enjoy a genre even when I know almost nothing about it
cards.description_short: Measures openness more than expertise.

cards.title: Silence is better than music I don't like
cards.description_short: Separates flexible listeners from people with stronger sonic boundaries.

cards.title: Songs get tied to specific people and seasons in my memory
cards.description_short: Shows whether music acts like an emotional time capsule for you.

decks.title: Food & Drinks
decks.description: From weeknight comfort meals to adventurous nights out, this deck maps how you eat, host, and indulge.

cards.title: Sushi
cards.description_short: A precise, beloved staple for people who like clean flavors and ritual.

cards.title: Tacos
cards.description_short: A flexible comfort food that works equally well messy, casual, and celebratory.

cards.title: Ramen
cards.description_short: A cozy bowl that can feel both restorative and indulgent.

cards.title: Thai curry
cards.description_short: A dish that balances spice, richness, herbs, and comfort.

cards.title: Fresh pasta
cards.description_short: Soft, rich, and handmade-feeling food that invites slowing down.

cards.title: Espresso
cards.description_short: A concentrated ritual for people who care how their day starts.

cards.title: Dim sum
cards.description_short: A shared-table meal that rewards variety, curiosity, and conversation.

cards.title: Brunch
cards.description_short: A social meal where food, coffee, and lingering all matter.

cards.title: Hot pot
cards.description_short: A communal meal where the experience is almost as important as the flavor.

cards.title: Mediterranean mezze
cards.description_short: A table of bright, shareable food built for lingering.

cards.title: Barbecue
cards.description_short: Deeply savory food with patience, smoke, and appetite baked in.

cards.title: Gelato
cards.description_short: Dessert that feels both indulgent and a little romantic.

cards.title: Farmers markets
cards.description_short: A food ritual built around seasonality, ingredients, and wandering.

cards.title: Natural wine
cards.description_short: A drink choice that can signal curiosity, taste, or a little chaos.

cards.title: Pho
cards.description_short: A brothy comfort meal that feels light and restorative at once.

cards.title: Shakshuka
cards.description_short: A bright, spiced dish that feels generous and casual.

cards.title: Craft beer
cards.description_short: A hobby-adjacent drink choice that can be playful, nerdy, or ritualistic.

cards.title: Pastries
cards.description_short: A small luxury that often turns coffee into a real event.

cards.title: Vegetarian tasting menus
cards.description_short: A food experience built on restraint, creativity, and surprise.

cards.title: Breakfast burritos
cards.description_short: Hearty, portable comfort food with very little pretense.

cards.title: I'd rather cook at home than go out for dinner
cards.description_short: Shows whether effort and intimacy matter more than convenience for you.

cards.title: I'm open to trying almost anything once
cards.description_short: Captures whether curiosity beats caution at the table.

cards.title: I care more about the company than the restaurant
cards.description_short: Reveals whether the setting or the shared energy matters more to you.

cards.title: I take my coffee routine a little too seriously
cards.description_short: Shows whether daily ritual matters as much as the flavor itself.

cards.title: Dessert can completely change my opinion of a meal
cards.description_short: Separates savory-first people from people who always leave room for sweetness.

cards.title: I like splitting plates so I can try more things
cards.description_short: Captures whether variety and sampling are part of how you enjoy food.

cards.title: I crave spicy food more than sweet food
cards.description_short: Shows where intensity usually wins for you.

cards.title: I enjoy hosting people for a home-cooked meal
cards.description_short: Reveals whether food is part of how you care for people.

cards.title: I'd rather have one great cocktail than three average ones
cards.description_short: Shows whether quality beats quantity when you order drinks.

cards.title: Breakfast food works for me at any hour
cards.description_short: Captures whether certain foods feel timelessly comforting to you.

cards.title: I notice when a place nails the small details like sauces or bread
cards.description_short: Reveals how much craft and care shape your experience of a meal.

cards.title: I can be convinced to leave the house for exceptional food
cards.description_short: Shows whether a meal can be an event in its own right for you.

cards.title: I like keeping a few reliable comfort meals in rotation
cards.description_short: Captures whether repetition feels grounding instead of boring to you.

cards.title: I'm the person who reads the full menu before arriving
cards.description_short: Shows whether anticipation and planning are part of the fun for you.

cards.title: I usually prefer food that feels fresh and light over rich and heavy
cards.description_short: Highlights how much energy and balance shape your taste.

cards.title: I enjoy cooking for other people more than cooking just for myself
cards.description_short: Reveals whether food feels relational, not just functional, for you.

cards.title: I'll travel across town for a dish I really love
cards.description_short: Shows whether food can be worth effort, ritual, and a little inconvenience.

cards.title: I like pairing food with a specific drink instead of ordering randomly
cards.description_short: Captures whether the full table experience matters to you.

cards.title: I want to know the best thing on the menu before I order
cards.description_short: Reveals whether you like guidance or would rather discover something yourself.

cards.title: A relaxed neighborhood spot beats a flashy restaurant for me
cards.description_short: Shows whether ease and atmosphere matter more than prestige for you.

decks.title: Travel
decks.description: Whether you plan every detail or chase the unexpected, this deck explores how you like to move through the world.

cards.title: Road trips
cards.description_short: A travel style built on playlists, detours, and the open-ended middle.

cards.title: Backpacking
cards.description_short: Budget travel that values freedom, stories, and low-friction movement.

cards.title: Beach resorts
cards.description_short: A trip shape centered on downtime, sun, and uncomplicated pleasure.

cards.title: National parks
cards.description_short: Travel that prioritizes awe, scale, and physical landscape.

cards.title: City breaks
cards.description_short: Short, energizing trips built around neighborhoods, food, and pace.

cards.title: Boutique hotels
cards.description_short: Places where design, atmosphere, and detail are part of the trip.

cards.title: All-inclusive resorts
cards.description_short: A simple travel mode where rest wins over logistics.

cards.title: Train travel through Europe
cards.description_short: A romantic, slower-paced way to move that makes transit part of the experience.

cards.title: Camping trips
cards.description_short: Nature-heavy travel that asks for flexibility and a tolerance for discomfort.

cards.title: Tokyo
cards.description_short: A city trip that rewards appetite, curiosity, and alertness.

cards.title: Iceland
cards.description_short: A landscape-first destination where nature often feels like the main character.

cards.title: Museum-heavy itineraries
cards.description_short: Trips structured around depth, context, and taking your time.

cards.title: Weekend cabin getaways
cards.description_short: Travel that trades novelty for reset, warmth, and a smaller rhythm.

cards.title: Hostels
cards.description_short: Shared-budget travel where spontaneity and social energy are built in.

cards.title: Scenic coastal drives
cards.description_short: Travel that values movement, views, and rhythm more than checklists.

cards.title: Food tours
cards.description_short: Trips shaped through appetite, local context, and social discovery.

cards.title: I plan almost every detail before I travel
cards.description_short: Shows whether preparation helps you relax or limits the fun.

cards.title: I'd rather explore somewhere nearby than take a long flight
cards.description_short: Reveals whether distance matters less than the feeling of getting away.

cards.title: I like having a loose plan and room to improvise
cards.description_short: Captures how much spontaneity you want inside a trip.

cards.title: I can handle discomfort if the experience feels worth it
cards.description_short: Shows whether payoff beats ease for you when you travel.

cards.title: I care more about the neighborhood than the hotel itself
cards.description_short: Reveals whether immersion matters more than polish for you.

cards.title: I pack light even if it means repeating outfits
cards.description_short: Captures whether mobility and ease matter more than options.

cards.title: I need downtime built into a trip or I burn out
cards.description_short: Shows whether recovery time is part of the ideal itinerary for you.

cards.title: I enjoy getting a little lost in a new place
cards.description_short: Reveals whether uncertainty feels exciting or stressful to you.

cards.title: I prefer one longer trip over several short ones
cards.description_short: Separates depth seekers from people who love quick bursts of novelty.

cards.title: I'd wake up early for a view even on vacation
cards.description_short: Shows whether experience beats comfort first thing in the morning.

cards.title: I love traveling with someone who can share the planning
cards.description_short: Reveals how collaborative you want trip logistics to feel.

cards.title: I usually remember the meals and conversations more than the landmarks
cards.description_short: Highlights whether emotional texture matters more than the checklist.

cards.title: I prefer private rooms over dorm-style stays
cards.description_short: Shows where privacy sits in your comfort hierarchy.

cards.title: I can be very happy on a low-budget trip if the energy is right
cards.description_short: Reveals whether mood and company beat polish for you.

cards.title: I enjoy revisiting places I already love
cards.description_short: Captures whether familiarity deepens travel instead of making it smaller.

cards.title: I like trips that mix structure with one adventurous wildcard
cards.description_short: Shows whether your sweet spot is balance rather than extremes.

cards.title: Nature excites me more than nightlife when I travel
cards.description_short: Separates scenery-driven trips from social-scene travel.

cards.title: I'd rather rent a car than rely fully on public transit
cards.description_short: Reveals whether autonomy matters more than local flow for you.

cards.title: I care more about how a trip feels than how it photographs
cards.description_short: Shows whether memory, ease, and meaning beat presentation for you.

cards.title: I like building a travel day around one standout experience
cards.description_short: Highlights whether focus feels better than trying to do everything.

cards.title: I'm comfortable taking a solo day even when I'm traveling with someone
cards.description_short: Reveals how much independence you like woven into shared travel.

cards.title: I prefer destinations with personality over ones that feel polished
cards.description_short: Separates charm seekers from perfection seekers.

cards.title: I don't need luxury, but I do need a clean place to sleep
cards.description_short: Shows where your comfort floor lives, even when you're flexible elsewhere.

cards.title: A good itinerary excites me more than airport shopping ever will
cards.description_short: Captures whether the trip itself begins in planning for you.

decks.title: Lifestyle & Routines
decks.description: This deck looks at the routines, rhythms, and habits that make daily life feel good to you.

cards.title: Morning walks
cards.description_short: A gentle routine that starts the day with movement and mental space.

cards.title: Strength training
cards.description_short: A structured habit that can feel grounding, energizing, and practical.

cards.title: Yoga classes
cards.description_short: Movement that blends physical effort with recovery and quiet attention.

cards.title: Sunday reset routines
cards.description_short: Weekly rituals built around order, preparation, and feeling ready again.

cards.title: Run clubs
cards.description_short: A habit that combines movement, routine, and a little social accountability.

cards.title: Crossword puzzles
cards.description_short: A small ritual for people who like quiet focus and incremental progress.

cards.title: I'm an early bird on most days
cards.description_short: Shows whether your natural rhythm leans toward mornings.

cards.title: I need quiet time before I feel social
cards.description_short: Reveals whether your day starts best with a little space.

cards.title: A few steady routines make my week feel better
cards.description_short: Captures whether consistency helps you feel more like yourself.

cards.title: I feel noticeably better when my space is tidy
cards.description_short: Shows how much order affects your mood and focus.

cards.title: I usually have more energy in the morning than at night
cards.description_short: Separates people who front-load their day from people who ramp up later.

cards.title: I need alone time to recharge after a busy stretch
cards.description_short: Reveals whether solitude feels like maintenance, not avoidance, for you.

cards.title: Exercise is one of the ways I manage stress
cards.description_short: Shows whether movement is tied to emotional regulation for you.

cards.title: A calm night in usually sounds better than a packed schedule
cards.description_short: Captures whether rest feels restorative or boring to you.

cards.title: I use a calendar or list to keep my life together
cards.description_short: Reveals whether external systems help you feel steady and capable.

cards.title: Small habits work better for me than big resolutions
cards.description_short: Shows whether you trust consistency over intensity.

cards.title: I'd rather have a comfortable home than an impressive one
cards.description_short: Highlights whether daily ease matters more than aesthetics or performance.

cards.title: I can be spontaneous, but I like knowing the basics of a plan
cards.description_short: Captures whether structure helps spontaneity feel easier for you.

cards.title: I get irritable when I'm overscheduled
cards.description_short: Reveals whether margin is emotionally important to your routine.

cards.title: Slow mornings feel luxurious to me
cards.description_short: Shows whether gentleness is part of your ideal rhythm.

cards.title: I almost always feel better after a workout, even if I resisted starting
cards.description_short: Captures whether effort reliably turns into energy for you.

cards.title: I would rather do chores steadily than let them pile up
cards.description_short: Shows whether maintenance feels easier when it's ordinary.

cards.title: I often need transition time after work before doing anything else
cards.description_short: Reveals whether your energy shifts need space instead of speed.

cards.title: I like hobbies that help me lose track of time
cards.description_short: Shows whether absorption and presence matter in how you recharge.

cards.title: A few dependable meals, shows, or rituals help me feel grounded
cards.description_short: Captures whether repetition can feel secure instead of stale.

cards.title: I feel better when at least one productive thing is planned for the day
cards.description_short: Shows whether momentum matters to your sense of ease.

cards.title: I can adapt to chaos, but I don't enjoy living in it
cards.description_short: Reveals whether flexibility and preference are different things for you.

cards.title: I try to schedule rest instead of just hoping for it
cards.description_short: Shows whether recovery needs intention to actually happen for you.

cards.title: I like feeling physically active even if it isn't in a gym
cards.description_short: Captures whether movement matters more than format for you.

cards.title: I usually know quickly when my routine is slipping
cards.description_short: Reveals how aware you are of your own rhythm and maintenance.

cards.title: I prefer realistic self-improvement over dramatic life overhauls
cards.description_short: Shows whether sustainable change matters more than reinvention.

cards.title: I like a mix of dependable rituals and occasional novelty
cards.description_short: Captures whether balance feels better than either extreme.

cards.title: I'm more likely to reflect at night than first thing in the morning
cards.description_short: Shows when self-awareness tends to open up for you.

cards.title: I try to protect my sleep when life gets busy
cards.description_short: Reveals whether rest is a priority or a casualty when things speed up.

cards.title: Consistency matters more to me than intensity in most areas of life
cards.description_short: Captures how you think about momentum, sustainability, and identity.

cards.title: I feel proud when I follow through on small promises to myself
cards.description_short: Shows whether internal trust is built through ordinary follow-through for you.

cards.title: I like days that have structure without feeling rigid
cards.description_short: Reveals whether freedom inside a frame is your ideal pace.

cards.title: I notice quickly when my energy is out of sync with my schedule
cards.description_short: Captures whether you track your own rhythms in a practical way.

cards.title: I'd rather improve one habit at a time than change everything at once
cards.description_short: Shows whether patience feels wiser than urgency to you.

cards.title: I feel most like myself when my week has a healthy rhythm
cards.description_short: Reveals how tied your identity is to pace, energy, and steady patterns.

decks.title: Social Habits
decks.description: From quiet dinners to crowded nights out, this deck explores how you spend your social energy.

cards.title: Game nights
cards.description_short: A social setting where activity keeps the energy playful and easy.

cards.title: Dinner parties
cards.description_short: A slower hang built on food, flow, and conversation.

cards.title: Karaoke nights
cards.description_short: A louder social setting where performance and silliness are part of the point.

cards.title: Rooftop bars
cards.description_short: A social scene that can feel breezy, performative, or energizing depending on the crowd.

cards.title: I prefer small gatherings over big parties
cards.description_short: Shows whether your social sweet spot is intimacy or scale.

cards.title: I'm usually the one who keeps the group chat alive
cards.description_short: Reveals whether you naturally sustain group energy between plans.

cards.title: I'd rather have one great conversation than a dozen shallow ones
cards.description_short: Captures whether depth matters more than variety for you socially.

cards.title: I need recovery time after a busy social weekend
cards.description_short: Shows whether connection costs energy as much as it gives it.

cards.title: I like being invited even if I'm not sure I'll say yes
cards.description_short: Reveals whether inclusion matters more than certainty for you.

cards.title: I'm comfortable introducing people who don't know each other
cards.description_short: Shows whether you naturally take on connector energy in a room.

cards.title: I'd rather host at home than fight for a table somewhere
cards.description_short: Captures whether control and comfort beat public energy for you.

cards.title: I leave events earlier than most people
cards.description_short: Reveals whether your social battery has a cleaner stop point than most.

cards.title: I warm up slowly in new groups
cards.description_short: Shows whether comfort takes time before your personality fully appears.

cards.title: I enjoy playful banter more than loud chaos
cards.description_short: Separates social playfulness from sensory overwhelm.

cards.title: I'm the friend who checks whether everyone got home safe
cards.description_short: Reveals whether caretaking is part of how you show up socially.

cards.title: I do better socially when I know at least one person well
cards.description_short: Shows whether anchor relationships make bigger settings easier for you.

cards.title: I like plans that have a clear start and end time
cards.description_short: Captures whether social structure feels relieving instead of restrictive.

cards.title: I enjoy meeting new people, but only in manageable doses
cards.description_short: Reveals whether novelty feels good until it starts draining you.

cards.title: I can hold my own at a party, but it's not where I feel most myself
cards.description_short: Separates capability from preference in social settings.

cards.title: I appreciate friends who understand that a canceled plan isn't personal
cards.description_short: Shows whether flexibility feels like care to you.

cards.title: I like a social setting more when there's an activity built in
cards.description_short: Reveals whether structured fun makes connection easier for you.

cards.title: I notice when one person in a group is being left out
cards.description_short: Captures whether you track social temperature and inclusion closely.

cards.title: I'm more likely to make plans if they involve food or a walk
cards.description_short: Shows whether simple, low-pressure plans are your easiest yes.

cards.title: I prefer one-on-one texting over constantly posting in a group chat
cards.description_short: Reveals whether intimacy feels more natural than broadcast-style connection.

cards.title: I enjoy being silly with people once I feel comfortable
cards.description_short: Shows how playfulness changes once trust is in the room.

cards.title: I can be friendly without wanting instant closeness
cards.description_short: Captures whether warmth and distance can comfortably coexist for you.

cards.title: I usually need context before I can enjoy a big event
cards.description_short: Reveals whether social ease rises when the structure is clearer.

cards.title: I like being the person who makes others feel welcome
cards.description_short: Shows whether hospitality feels energizing, not just dutiful, for you.

cards.title: I'd rather catch up with two close friends than attend a huge event
cards.description_short: Separates meaningful closeness from social scale.

cards.title: I'm okay saying no when my social battery is done
cards.description_short: Reveals whether limits are easy or hard for you to name.

cards.title: I can handle small talk, but I don't want to stay there forever
cards.description_short: Shows whether surface-level ease is enough or just a bridge for you.

cards.title: I like friends who can laugh together without constant performance
cards.description_short: Captures whether ease matters more than spectacle in shared fun.

cards.title: I don't need to be out every weekend to feel connected
cards.description_short: Reveals whether frequency matters less than quality of contact for you.

cards.title: I enjoy a little chaos if the people feel safe
cards.description_short: Shows whether trust changes your appetite for louder energy.

cards.title: I tend to remember how people made the room feel
cards.description_short: Captures whether emotional atmosphere sticks with you more than details.

cards.title: I appreciate direct communication more than guessing games
cards.description_short: Shows whether clarity matters especially in social dynamics for you.

cards.title: I like events that leave room to wander between conversations
cards.description_short: Reveals whether flexible social flow feels better than fixed intensity.

cards.title: I care more about emotional ease than social status in a room
cards.description_short: Shows what you scan for first when you enter a space with other people.

cards.title: I'm happiest when plans feel intentional, not obligatory
cards.description_short: Captures whether choice and meaning matter more than just showing up.

cards.title: I value friends who can do deep talk and nonsense in the same night
cards.description_short: Shows whether emotional depth and lightness both matter for chemistry.

decks.title: Humor
decks.description: The jokes you love and the way you laugh can reveal a lot about chemistry, comfort, and timing.

cards.title: The Office
cards.description_short: A comedy benchmark for people who love awkward timing and tiny reactions.

cards.title: Parks and Recreation
cards.description_short: Warm, optimistic comedy that still knows how to land a joke.

cards.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
cards.description_short: Fast, playful humor with a little absurdity and a lot of affection.

cards.title: Derry Girls
cards.description_short: Chaos, speed, and emotional warmth wrapped in sharp comedy.

cards.title: John Mulaney
cards.description_short: Story-driven stand-up where specificity turns into something universal.

cards.title: Key & Peele
cards.description_short: Sketch comedy that swings between clever, physical, and surreal.

cards.title: Schitt's Creek
cards.description_short: Dry comedy that becomes unexpectedly tender over time.

cards.title: I Think You Should Leave
cards.description_short: An absurdist comedy built for people who like discomfort pushed just a bit too far.

cards.title: Taskmaster
cards.description_short: Playful competition where silliness and wit matter more than winning.

cards.title: Nathan For You
cards.description_short: A dry, strange comedy that gets funnier the more serious it looks.

cards.title: Fleabag
cards.description_short: Comedy that weaponizes self-awareness, timing, and emotional mess.

cards.title: Hot Fuzz
cards.description_short: A densely packed movie that keeps rewarding attention and rewatching.

cards.title: The Good Place
cards.description_short: A rare comedy where philosophy and punchlines actually help each other.

cards.title: Tig Notaro
cards.description_short: Low-key stand-up where understatement becomes part of the joke.

cards.title: New Girl
cards.description_short: Character comedy that stays light without feeling empty.

cards.title: The Onion
cards.description_short: Deadpan satire that only works when the delivery never blinks.

cards.title: Bob's Burgers
cards.description_short: A gentle, slightly weird show that loves wordplay and family chaos.

cards.title: What We Do in the Shadows
cards.description_short: Deadpan supernatural comedy that stays funny by staying committed to the bit.

cards.title: Abbott Elementary
cards.description_short: Humor rooted in character affection and little moments of recognition.

cards.title: Paddington 2
cards.description_short: A sweet comedy that uses physical bits without losing emotional warmth.

cards.title: I appreciate dark humor when it's clever, not cruel
cards.description_short: Shows where your line is between bold and mean.

cards.title: I use humor to diffuse tension when things get awkward
cards.description_short: Reveals whether jokes feel like comfort, defense, or both for you.

cards.title: I love a well-timed deadpan response
cards.description_short: Captures whether restraint makes comedy sharper for you.

cards.title: Puns annoy me until they make me laugh
cards.description_short: Shows whether you resist or surrender to wordplay.

cards.title: I'm more likely to laugh at something absurd than something polished
cards.description_short: Reveals whether surprise beats craft for you in the moment.

cards.title: I like humor that feels a little too specific
cards.description_short: Captures whether oddly precise jokes make you feel seen.

cards.title: I don't fully trust people who can't laugh at themselves
cards.description_short: Shows whether self-awareness and playfulness feel linked for you.

cards.title: Sarcasm works for me if it feels affectionate
cards.description_short: Reveals whether sharpness is fun or tiring depending on tone.

cards.title: I'm easily won over by physical comedy
cards.description_short: Shows whether a great visual bit can override almost anything else.

cards.title: I'd rather laugh with someone than laugh at someone
cards.description_short: Captures whether warmth changes what feels funny to you.

cards.title: I like jokes that reward paying attention
cards.description_short: Reveals whether layered humor feels more satisfying than obvious humor.

cards.title: I can enjoy very silly humor if the commitment is real
cards.description_short: Shows whether sincerity inside nonsense works for you.

cards.title: I notice when humor is hiding discomfort
cards.description_short: Captures whether you hear the subtext under the laugh.

cards.title: I like people who can keep a straight face while saying something ridiculous
cards.description_short: Shows whether surprise and control are part of what makes you laugh.

cards.title: I laugh hardest in intimate settings, not when I'm trying to perform wit
cards.description_short: Reveals whether ease matters more than audience in your humor.

cards.title: I bond fastest with people who make me laugh unexpectedly
cards.description_short: Captures whether surprise feels like chemistry for you.

cards.title: I enjoy humor that's a little weird but not mean
cards.description_short: Shows where your line is between strange and hostile.

cards.title: I can appreciate a brutal one-liner if everyone feels safe
cards.description_short: Reveals whether context changes how sharp humor lands for you.

cards.title: I love when a joke sounds casual but lands perfectly
cards.description_short: Captures whether subtle delivery matters as much as the joke itself.

cards.title: I probably use humor as a love language more than I admit
cards.description_short: Shows whether joking feels like one of your easiest forms of affection.

decks.title: Relationship Preferences
decks.description: This deck explores the habits, boundaries, and forms of closeness you want in a relationship.

cards.title: Weekly date nights
cards.description_short: A ritual of intentional time that makes the relationship feel actively chosen.

cards.title: Good morning texts
cards.description_short: A tiny contact point that can feel sweet, grounding, or excessive.

cards.title: Quality time matters more to me than grand gestures
cards.description_short: Shows whether ordinary closeness beats occasional spectacle for you.

cards.title: I need honest feedback even when it's hard to hear
cards.description_short: Reveals whether truth feels more loving than comfort for you.

cards.title: Physical touch matters a lot to me
cards.description_short: Captures how much affection needs a physical dimension to feel complete.

cards.title: I want a relationship where both people still have independent lives
cards.description_short: Shows whether closeness and individuality need to coexist clearly for you.

cards.title: I feel safest when expectations are stated clearly
cards.description_short: Reveals whether ambiguity feels romantic or stressful to you.

cards.title: Consistency matters more to me than intensity
cards.description_short: Captures whether steadiness feels more meaningful than peaks and valleys.

cards.title: I'd rather talk through tension than pretend it isn't there
cards.description_short: Shows whether avoidance feels calmer or more costly to you.

cards.title: I appreciate affection that feels natural, not performative
cards.description_short: Reveals whether sincerity matters more than volume in care.

cards.title: I need a partner who can apologize without getting defensive
cards.description_short: Captures whether accountability feels essential to safety for you.

cards.title: I like checking in after conflict instead of just moving on
cards.description_short: Shows whether repair needs follow-through for you to trust it.

cards.title: I want a relationship where both people cheer for each other's growth
cards.description_short: Reveals whether mutual expansion matters as much as mutual comfort to you.

cards.title: I need some alone time even in a close relationship
cards.description_short: Captures whether breathing room feels regulating instead of distant.

cards.title: I feel loved when someone remembers the small things about me
cards.description_short: Shows whether attention to detail reads as care for you.

cards.title: I prefer direct conversations over passive hints
cards.description_short: Reveals whether clarity matters more than soft delivery for you.

cards.title: I don't need constant contact, but I do need reliability
cards.description_short: Captures whether steadiness feels different from frequency for you.

cards.title: I value emotional safety over being constantly impressed
cards.description_short: Shows whether security feels more romantic than intensity to you.

cards.title: I want affection to feel reciprocal, not one-sided
cards.description_short: Reveals whether balance is part of what makes closeness feel real.

cards.title: Conflict can be healthy if both people stay respectful
cards.description_short: Captures whether disagreement feels threatening or potentially useful to you.

cards.title: I notice quickly when someone avoids uncomfortable conversations
cards.description_short: Shows how much emotional avoidance stands out to you.

cards.title: I want a partner who can be playful and serious with me
cards.description_short: Reveals whether range matters more than a single strong mode of closeness.

cards.title: Shared routines can make a relationship feel more grounded
cards.description_short: Captures whether small repeated rituals deepen intimacy for you.

cards.title: I care less about perfect chemistry than about how we repair after friction
cards.description_short: Shows whether durability matters more than immediate ease for you.

cards.title: I appreciate reassurance, but I don't want to rely on it for every doubt
cards.description_short: Reveals how you balance comfort with self-trust.

cards.title: I want both people to have room for changing goals and identities
cards.description_short: Captures whether flexibility feels like growth or instability to you.

cards.title: Being chosen openly matters more to me than vague mixed signals
cards.description_short: Shows whether certainty reads as care for you.

cards.title: I like relationships where support feels practical, not just verbal
cards.description_short: Reveals whether effort is clearest when it becomes action for you.

cards.title: I value a partner who can tell the truth kindly
cards.description_short: Captures whether honesty and gentleness need to travel together for you.

cards.title: Emotional steadiness attracts me more than emotional chaos
cards.description_short: Shows whether calm feels compelling or dull to you.

cards.title: I like affection that shows up in everyday habits, not only big moments
cards.description_short: Reveals whether care feels most real when it is ordinary and repeated.

cards.title: I don't expect perfect agreement, but I do expect respect
cards.description_short: Captures whether difference feels manageable when tone stays clean.

cards.title: I want both closeness and breathing room
cards.description_short: Shows whether intimacy and independence feel equally important to you.

cards.title: I appreciate people who can name what they need without punishment
cards.description_short: Reveals whether emotional clarity feels safe and attractive to you.

cards.title: Trust grows faster for me when actions match words
cards.description_short: Captures whether consistency is the quickest route to closeness for you.

cards.title: I want a relationship that feels like a team without losing individuality
cards.description_short: Shows whether partnership and autonomy both need visible room in your life.

cards.title: I care about how we disagree as much as how we get along
cards.description_short: Reveals whether process matters as much as compatibility for you.

cards.title: I'd rather build something steady than chase something dramatic
cards.description_short: Captures whether consistency feels more romantic than volatility.

cards.title: I appreciate warmth, but I also need room to be honest
cards.description_short: Shows whether softness and truth have to coexist for you to stay open.

cards.title: A relationship feels strongest to me when both people feel free and committed
cards.description_short: Reveals whether security and choice are both part of your ideal bond.

decks.title: Values
decks.description: From honesty to ambition to compassion, this deck explores what feels non-negotiable in how you live.

cards.title: Honesty matters more to me than being liked
cards.description_short: Shows whether truth feels worth social discomfort to you.

cards.title: Loyalty means a lot to me, especially when life gets messy
cards.description_short: Reveals whether steadiness under stress feels deeply important to you.

cards.title: I admire ambition when it's paired with kindness
cards.description_short: Captures whether drive needs softness to feel admirable to you.

cards.title: Family can mean chosen people, not only relatives
cards.description_short: Shows how expansively you define belonging and loyalty.

cards.title: Freedom matters to me more than fitting into a script
cards.description_short: Reveals how strongly autonomy shapes your decisions.

cards.title: Creativity feels essential, not optional
cards.description_short: Shows whether making or imagining things feels central to your life.

cards.title: Fairness is more important to me than convenience
cards.description_short: Captures whether principle should still matter when it is inconvenient.

cards.title: I believe people can change if they're willing to do the work
cards.description_short: Reveals whether you see growth as real, limited, or unlikely.

cards.title: Stability is underrated and deeply attractive to me
cards.description_short: Shows whether steadiness feels inspiring rather than boring to you.

cards.title: Compassion matters most when it's inconvenient
cards.description_short: Captures whether values only count when they cost something.

cards.title: Some traditions are worth keeping if they still serve people
cards.description_short: Reveals whether tradition feels grounding, restrictive, or both.

cards.title: Progress means little to me if it costs basic humanity
cards.description_short: Shows how you balance optimism with moral limits.

cards.title: I respect people who hold strong values without acting superior
cards.description_short: Captures whether humility matters as much as conviction to you.

cards.title: I care more about integrity than image
cards.description_short: Reveals whether private character matters more than public impression.

cards.title: Ambition is healthiest when it leaves room for relationships
cards.description_short: Shows how you think success should relate to the rest of life.

cards.title: I'd rather be around someone dependable than impressive
cards.description_short: Captures whether reliability feels more meaningful than charisma to you.

cards.title: Personal freedom matters, but so does responsibility
cards.description_short: Reveals whether freedom feels incomplete without accountability.

cards.title: I pay attention to how people treat service workers and strangers
cards.description_short: Shows whether everyday fairness feels more revealing than grand statements.

cards.title: I'm drawn to people who can admit when they were wrong
cards.description_short: Captures whether humility is part of what earns your respect.

cards.title: Kindness tells me more about a person than charm does
cards.description_short: Reveals whether warmth feels more trustworthy than polish for you.

cards.title: I feel best when my values show up in small daily choices
cards.description_short: Shows whether principles feel most real in ordinary life for you.

cards.title: I think boundaries can be a form of compassion
cards.description_short: Captures whether protection and care feel compatible to you.

cards.title: I'm skeptical of ambition that requires constant self-abandonment
cards.description_short: Reveals whether success should still leave room for a whole life.

cards.title: I value people who can stay soft without becoming passive
cards.description_short: Shows whether tenderness and strength need to coexist for you.

cards.title: Tradition feels meaningful when it creates belonging, not pressure
cards.description_short: Captures what makes inherited rituals feel alive instead of heavy.

cards.title: Progress should make ordinary life better, not just louder
cards.description_short: Reveals whether your politics of improvement are practical, symbolic, or both.

cards.title: I care about whether someone is fair when nobody is watching
cards.description_short: Shows whether quiet integrity matters more than declared principle to you.

cards.title: Creativity is one of the ways I understand who I am
cards.description_short: Captures whether expression feels like identity rather than decoration.

cards.title: I value steadiness more as I get older
cards.description_short: Reveals whether your priorities are moving toward calm or staying open-ended.

cards.title: Loyalty without honesty isn't enough for me
cards.description_short: Shows whether allegiance still needs truth to feel real to you.

cards.title: People reveal their values through what they protect
cards.description_short: Captures whether attention, resources, and defense say more than language to you.

cards.title: I don't need everyone to agree with me, but I do need respect
cards.description_short: Reveals how much difference you can tolerate when the tone stays clean.

cards.title: I trust slow growth more than dramatic reinvention
cards.description_short: Shows whether your faith sits more with steady effort than huge pivots.

cards.title: Mercy and accountability can exist together
cards.description_short: Captures whether your moral world leaves room for nuance under pressure.

cards.title: I admire people who can be ambitious without becoming rigid
cards.description_short: Shows whether adaptability matters inside excellence for you.

cards.title: Security matters to me, but I never want it to become fear
cards.description_short: Reveals how you think about safety without shrinking your life.

cards.title: Family loyalty shouldn't excuse harmful behavior
cards.description_short: Captures whether closeness ever outweighs accountability for you.

cards.title: I like values that make life feel bigger, not smaller
cards.description_short: Shows whether your principles expand possibility or mainly create limits.

cards.title: Compassion without boundaries turns into resentment
cards.description_short: Reveals whether you believe care needs shape to stay healthy.

cards.title: The people I trust most are consistent in public and private
cards.description_short: Shows whether coherence matters more than charm in your moral read of people.

decks.title: Communication Style
decks.description: This deck explores how you listen, process, clarify, and say what you mean.

cards.title: Voice notes
cards.description_short: A communication mode that sits somewhere between texting and calling.

cards.title: Long walks for serious talks
cards.description_short: A setting where movement can make vulnerability feel more possible.

cards.title: I prefer texting for everyday check-ins
cards.description_short: Shows whether low-pressure written contact feels most natural to you.

cards.title: If something matters, I'd rather talk face to face
cards.description_short: Reveals whether depth requires presence for you.

cards.title: I need time to process before I respond in a hard conversation
cards.description_short: Captures whether speed makes you clearer or less honest.

cards.title: I say what I mean pretty directly
cards.description_short: Shows whether clarity and bluntness are close neighbors for you or not.

cards.title: I appreciate people who ask clarifying questions instead of guessing
cards.description_short: Reveals whether curiosity feels more loving than assumption to you.

cards.title: Tone matters to me almost as much as content
cards.description_short: Shows whether delivery changes the entire meaning of what gets said for you.

cards.title: I'm usually more thoughtful in writing than in the moment
cards.description_short: Captures whether distance helps your real thoughts come into focus.

cards.title: I'd rather address tension early than let it simmer
cards.description_short: Reveals whether discomfort feels better to face now or later.

cards.title: I listen for what someone means, not only what they say
cards.description_short: Shows whether subtext feels as important as the words themselves.

cards.title: I can go quiet when I'm overwhelmed, even if I still care
cards.description_short: Captures whether retreat is about indifference or overload for you.

cards.title: I value people who can be direct without being harsh
cards.description_short: Reveals whether honesty and gentleness need to coexist for you.

cards.title: I don't love phone calls without warning
cards.description_short: Shows whether surprise access feels intimate or intrusive to you.

cards.title: I usually know when a conversation needs softness instead of solutions
cards.description_short: Captures whether emotional timing comes naturally to you.

cards.title: I want hard conversations to feel collaborative, not adversarial
cards.description_short: Reveals whether disagreement feels safest when it still feels like a team effort.

cards.title: I communicate best when I feel like there's enough time to think
cards.description_short: Shows whether pace changes your clarity and honesty.

cards.title: I don't mind disagreement if the respect stays intact
cards.description_short: Captures whether conflict feels energizing, neutral, or draining to you.

cards.title: I can be very candid once I feel safe
cards.description_short: Shows whether trust changes how direct you allow yourself to be.

cards.title: I notice quickly when someone is only waiting for their turn to speak
cards.description_short: Reveals how sensitive you are to the difference between hearing and listening.

cards.title: I prefer one honest conversation over a week of weird energy
cards.description_short: Captures whether ambiguity feels manageable or exhausting to you.

cards.title: I like when someone can summarize what they heard before reacting
cards.description_short: Shows whether feeling understood is a core part of feeling safe for you.

cards.title: I soften my message when I'm worried my honesty will land badly
cards.description_short: Reveals whether care changes how directly you speak.

cards.title: I appreciate direct questions more than vague fishing
cards.description_short: Captures whether explicitness feels more respectful than subtle probing for you.

cards.title: I need room to change my mind mid-conversation
cards.description_short: Shows whether you think aloud or arrive with a finished answer.

cards.title: If a topic is emotional, I want less performance and more honesty
cards.description_short: Reveals whether sincerity matters more than perfect wording for you.

cards.title: I'm comfortable saying, 'I need a minute to think'
cards.description_short: Shows whether you can name your process in real time.

cards.title: I care about whether someone can receive feedback without immediately debating it
cards.description_short: Captures how important receptivity feels to you in hard conversations.

cards.title: I like people who can be playful and sincere in the same conversation
cards.description_short: Reveals whether range feels like safety and chemistry to you.

cards.title: I prefer a calm text over a dramatic paragraph
cards.description_short: Shows whether regulation matters more than volume in written conflict for you.

cards.title: I'm more open when someone makes it clear they're trying to understand
cards.description_short: Captures how much intention changes your willingness to share.

cards.title: I don't need perfect wording; I need real intent
cards.description_short: Reveals whether sincerity outranks polish for you.

cards.title: I can be indirect when I'm unsure how my honesty will land
cards.description_short: Shows whether caution or clarity wins when the stakes feel high.

cards.title: I appreciate people who can ask for reassurance clearly
cards.description_short: Captures whether emotional needs feel easier to meet when they are named plainly.

cards.title: I notice subtext, but I don't want to rely on it
cards.description_short: Shows whether you want clarity even when you can read between the lines.

cards.title: I feel closer to people who can stay curious in disagreement
cards.description_short: Reveals whether curiosity changes how safe conflict feels to you.

cards.title: I'd rather repair a misunderstanding than pretend it didn't happen
cards.description_short: Shows whether closure matters enough for you to re-open discomfort.

cards.title: I need communication that feels grounded, not frantic
cards.description_short: Captures whether regulation changes how much you can hear and say.

cards.title: I respect people who can be both assertive and tender
cards.description_short: Reveals whether strength feels best when it stays connected to care for you.

cards.title: The best conversations for me feel honest, attentive, and unforced
cards.description_short: Shows whether ease and depth need to arrive together for a conversation to feel good.
